tasks.title: Scaffold project, tooling & config
tasks.description: Create the TypeScript + discord.js v14 project skeleton with full tooling and config. package.json (npm) with scripts: dev (tsx watch src/index.ts), build (tsc), typecheck (tsc --noEmit), test (vitest run), lint (eslint . --ext .ts), format (prettier --write .), start (node dist/index.js), deploy (tsx src/deploy-commands.ts). tsconfig strict. eslint + prettier + vitest configs. .env.example listing DISCORD_TOKEN, DISCORD_CLIENT_ID, DEFAULT_TIMEZONE, DEV_GUILD_ID, DATABASE_PATH, MAX_EVENT_DAYS, MAX_RESULT_WINDOWS (names only). README. src/config.ts loads dotenv + validates required vars (throws naming the bad var; DEFAULT_TIMEZONE validated as IANA via luxon). Acceptance: all npm scripts exist and exit 0 on the scaffold; config throws on missing/invalid var and succeeds on valid (unit-tested by injecting env); .env gitignored. Reuse the repo's existing .gitignore (already present); extend, don't overwrite.
acceptance: all npm scripts exist and pass on scaffold
config validates required env and throws naming the bad var (unit-tested)
tasks.modelReason: sonnet — standard TS/discord.js scaffold from explicit criteria; foundational but low-ambiguity

tasks.title: Bot bootstrap, interaction router & command deploy
tasks.description: src/index.ts builds a discord.js v14 Client (minimal intents for slash commands), logs in with DISCORD_TOKEN, logs 'logged in as <tag>' on ready. Implement an interaction router (src/interactions/) that auto-discovers command handlers (src/interactions/commands/*) and button/modal handlers (src/interactions/buttons/*, by customId prefix) via DIRECTORY SCAN so adding a handler file needs NO shared-file edit (prevents later merge conflicts). src/deploy-commands.ts registers all discovered command builders — guild-scoped when DEV_GUILD_ID set (instant) else global. Unknown interactions ignored gracefully; no token => exits with the config error, not an unhandled crash.
acceptance: client logs in and logs ready
router dispatches command/button/modal via directory-scan registry
tasks.modelReason: sonnet — discord.js wiring with a registry pattern; some design but well-understood

tasks.title: SQLite persistence layer & domain types
tasks.description: src/db/connection.ts opens better-sqlite3 at DATABASE_PATH (default ./data/scheduler.sqlite), creates parent dir, WAL + foreign_keys ON. Idempotent schema (events, participants, busy_entries with FKs + ON DELETE CASCADE) per the feature's data model. src/db/repo.ts is the ONLY place with SQL; typed functions: createEvent, getEvent, setEventMessageId, addParticipant, removeParticipant(cascades busy), listParticipants, addBusyEntry, listBusyEntries(eventId,userId?), removeBusyEntry, clearBusyEntries(eventId,userId), deleteEvent. Shared types Event, Participant, BusyEntry, BusyKind in src/domain/types.ts (no discord.js). Vitest against a temp/in-memory DB covering CRUD + cascade. Authorization note: repo writes are keyed by (event_id,user_id); callers pass the Discord user id from the interaction, never a client value.
tasks.modelReason: sonnet — clear schema + repo from the feature file; reliability covered by tests

tasks.title: Pure validation module (dates, ranges, times, event)
tasks.description: src/domain/validate.ts — ALL pure validators (built once so create/busy-dates/busy-times import without editing this file): validateEventInput (title 1-100; start/end YYYY-MM-DD; start<=end; span<=MAX_EVENT_DAYS; timezone valid IANA via luxon), validateDateRange (parse; from<=to; clamp to event [start,end]; reject if fully outside), validateTimeRange (HH:MM 24h; start<end; dates in range; note times outside 10:00-24:00 have no effect). Each returns a typed ok/error with a specific message. Heavy vitest coverage incl. boundaries and clamping. Restates FEAT-event-lifecycle-1 + availability-input-1/2 validation criteria.
acceptance: event-input validation incl. span cap + IANA tz
tasks.modelReason: sonnet — pure validation with explicit rules; consolidated to avoid a shared-file conflict

tasks.title: customId encode/decode helpers
tasks.description: src/interactions/customId.ts — encode/decode the button customIds 'evt:<action>:<eventId>' (actions: accept, setbusy, mybusy, results, leave). Round-trip (action,eventId); reject malformed ids. Pure; unit-tested. The decoded eventId is the only thing trusted from a customId — never a user id (the acting user comes from interaction.user.id).
tasks.modelReason: sonnet — tiny pure helper but used by every button; keep reliable

tasks.title: Slot grid (pure, timezone/DST-safe)
tasks.description: src/domain/constants.ts (DAY_START_MIN=600, DAY_END_MIN=1440, SLOT_MINUTES=30). src/domain/slots.ts: buildSlots(startDate,endDate,timezone) -> ordered slots {date,startMin,endMin,startInstant,endInstant} covering 10:00->24:00 in 30-min steps per date inclusive (28/normal day; first 10:00, last 23:30-24:00; never before 10:00), instants via luxon DateTime.fromObject({...},{zone}). isBusyEntryOverlappingSlot(entry,slot): date/date_range busy for all slots on those dates; time_range busy for slots overlapping [start_time,end_time) on applicable dates (a slot touching only the end is NOT busy). DST-correct (wall-clock derived, not fixed ms). Vitest: 28-slot day, multi-day, each overlap kind incl. boundaries, a US DST date. Uses BusyEntry type from src/domain/types.ts (T-003).
acceptance: overlap correct for each entry kind incl. boundaries
tasks.modelReason: opus — timezone/DST correctness is subtle and is the core the engine relies on (model_hint: opus)

tasks.title: Event message embed + buttons + live update
tasks.description: src/ui/eventMessage.ts: build the event embed (title, date range, timezone, creator, participant mentions + count, usage footer) and an action row using customIds from T-004 (Accept evt:accept, Set busy times evt:setbusy, My busy times evt:mybusy, Results evt:results, Leave evt:leave). updateEventMessage(client,event) edits the existing message by message_id from current event+participants (never reposts; no-op if deleted). Empty roster => 'No one yet — click Accept'. Respect embed limits. Reuse repo (T-003) for participant data and customId (T-004).
tasks.modelReason: sonnet — embed/button rendering from explicit criteria

tasks.title: Matching & ranking engine (pure)
tasks.description: src/domain/matching.ts: computeAvailability(participants,busyEntries,startDate,endDate,timezone,options?) -> {windows:RankedWindow[],perDay:DayAvailability[]}. Per slot (from T-011) compute free set/count; merge consecutive same-free-set slots on a date into RankedWindow {date,startMin,endMin,freeCount,freeUserIds,totalParticipants}; rank by freeCount desc -> duration desc -> earliest; return top MAX_RESULT_WINDOWS (default 5); everyone-free first by construction; never before 10:00/after 24:00. perDay: per-date per-slot freeCount array (heatmap). Pure/deterministic. Heavy vitest: everyone-free, partial overlaps -> distinct windows, ranking + tie-breaks, zero participants, one fully-busy capping N-1, busy date_range blocking days, multi-day.
tasks.modelReason: opus — core ranking correctness, many edge cases, high blast radius (model_hint: opus)

tasks.title: /plan create-event command
tasks.description: src/interactions/commands/plan.ts: /plan with options title, start, end, timezone(optional->DEFAULT_TIMEZONE). Validate via T-006 validateEventInput; invalid => ephemeral error naming the problem, no row created. Valid => repo.createEvent, post the event message (T-005) in the channel, setEventMessageId, ephemeral confirmation linking the message. Defer if needed for the 3s ack. Self-registers via the directory-scan registry (do NOT edit deploy-commands.ts). Creator is NOT auto-added as participant.
acceptance: valid -> event row + posted message + confirmation
tasks.modelReason: sonnet — command handler wiring validated pieces together

tasks.title: Participation buttons (Accept / Leave)
tasks.description: src/interactions/buttons/accept.ts + leave.ts (self-register via the button registry). Accept (evt:accept:<id>): add interaction.user.id as participant (idempotent; already-in => ephemeral note); update event message (T-005); ephemeral confirm. Leave (evt:leave:<id>): remove the caller + cascade their busy entries; non-participant => ephemeral note; update message; ephemeral confirm. The acting user is ALWAYS interaction.user.id, never from the customId.
acceptance: Accept adds participant idempotently + updates message
Leave removes caller + clears their busy + updates message
tasks.modelReason: sonnet — button handlers over repo + message update

tasks.title: Results display (ranked windows + heatmap, refresh)
tasks.description: src/ui/results.ts renders an embed: ranked top windows ('Tue Jul 1 · 2:00-5:00 PM · all 5 free' / '4/5 free — missing @user') + a compact per-day availability bar from perDay freeCounts with a legend; times in the event timezone. src/interactions/commands/results.ts (/results) and src/interactions/buttons/results.ts (evt:results) compute via T-012 from current data and show the embed ephemerally. Export refreshResults(event) for input features to call. Empty states: no participants; participants but no all-free window (show best near-misses, never empty when any slot has >=1 free). Respect embed limits (trim to MAX_RESULT_WINDOWS; heatmap fits a 31-day event). Pure formatting helpers (window->string, glyph mapping) unit-tested.
acceptance: ranked windows + heatmap embed in event tz
tasks.modelReason: sonnet — rendering over the engine output; formatting helpers tested

tasks.title: Busy dates & ranges input
tasks.description: src/interactions/buttons/setBusy.ts (evt:setbusy:<id>): ephemeral entry point presenting the busy-dates and busy-time options. src/interactions/commands/busyDates.ts (/busy-dates from, optional to): validate via T-006 validateDateRange (clamp to event range); require the caller be a participant (repo.listParticipants; else ephemeral 'Accept first'); store kind date/date_range keyed to interaction.user.id; call refreshResults (T-014); ephemeral summary. Self-register via registries.
tasks.modelReason: sonnet — input handler over validate + repo + refresh

tasks.title: Busy time ranges input
tasks.description: src/interactions/commands/busyTime.ts (/busy-time from_date, optional to_date, start_time, end_time): validate via T-006 validateTimeRange (HH:MM, start<end, dates in range); applies to each day in span; participant-gated (else ephemeral 'Accept first'); store kind time_range keyed to interaction.user.id; call refreshResults (T-014); ephemeral confirm. If a modal is used, <=5 inputs. Self-register via registry.
acceptance: time range on a date or date span stored
tasks.modelReason: sonnet — input handler analogous to T-009

tasks.title: Manage my busy times (view / remove / clear)
tasks.description: src/interactions/buttons/myBusy.ts (evt:mybusy:<id>) and/or /mybusy: ephemeral list of the CALLER's busy entries for the event (human-readable in event tz) via src/ui/busyList.ts, each with a Remove control + Clear all. Remove deletes one; Clear all deletes all the caller's entries; both call refreshResults (T-014) and re-render the ephemeral list. Empty => 'You have no busy times set'. A user only ever sees/affects their own entries (keyed to interaction.user.id). Pure list-formatting helpers unit-tested where present.
tasks.modelReason: sonnet — management UI over repo + refresh
